title: HPC Profiler Event Schema
description: Shared event schema for CPU and GPU profiler streams. Version 0.1 — Phase 0 contract.

EventType.description: The kind of profiling event. Shared across CPU and GPU events.

TransferKind.description: Direction of a GPU memory transfer.

BaseEvent.description: Fields present on every event regardless of source.

event_id.description: Monotonically increasing integer. Unique within a single profiling session.

timestamp_ns.description: Wall-clock time in nanoseconds since session start. Used for temporal correlation.

process_id.description: OS process ID of the profiled process.

thread_id.description: OS thread ID. Used to distinguish concurrent allocation streams.

CpuEvent.description: A memory event captured by the Python profiler. Emitted on allocation, deallocation, or GC collection.

alloc_address.description: Memory address of the Python object. PRIMARY correlation key — matched against GPU src_address and dst_address.

alloc_size_bytes.description: Size of the allocation in bytes. Used as secondary correlation key when address matching is ambiguous.

object_type.description: Python type qualname of the allocated object, e.g. 'numpy.ndarray', 'Tensor', 'list'.

module_name.description: Python module where the type is defined, e.g. 'numpy', 'torch', 'builtins'.

ref_count.description: Python reference count at the moment of this event.

callstack.description: Python call stack at time of allocation, outermost frame first. Captured via tracemalloc.

gc_generation.description: Python GC generation (0, 1, or 2). Indicates object age and collection frequency.

is_dealloc.description: True if this event represents a deallocation. Pairs with an earlier ALLOC event at the same alloc_address.

peak_rss_kb.description: Process RSS in kilobytes at the time of this collect() tick. 0 on DEALLOC events.

lifetime_ns.description: Nanoseconds between ALLOC and DEALLOC for this address. Populated on DEALLOC events only; 0 on ALLOC.

is_numpy_buffer.description: True if the object exposes __array_interface__ or __cuda_array_interface__ (numpy, cupy, torch). The real allocation size is buffer_nbytes, not alloc_size_bytes.

buffer_nbytes.description: obj.nbytes when is_numpy_buffer is True; the C-level buffer size tracemalloc cannot see. 0 otherwise.

parent_address.description: id() of the first tracked GC referrer of this object, or 0 if none found or track_refs=False.

pinned_address.description: Actual C-level data pointer for buffer objects (numpy/cupy/torch). For a numpy array this is __array_interface__['data'][0]; for torch it is tensor.data_ptr(). CUPTI reports this address as src_address on HOST_TO_DEVICE transfers, enabling HARD correlation via Pass 1b. 0 for non-buffer objects.

tracemalloc_size.description: Size in bytes as directly reported by tracemalloc for this traceback (cumulative, not just the diff). Useful when alloc_size_bytes from the snapshot diff under-reports due to traceback aggregation. 0 on DEALLOC events.

array_shape.description: Dimensions of the array for numpy/cupy/torch objects, e.g. [512, 64]. Empty list for non-array objects.

array_dtype.description: Data type string for array objects, e.g. 'float32', 'int64'. Empty string for non-array objects.

GpuEvent.description: A memory event captured by the GPU profiler via CUPTI. Emitted on memory transfer, page fault, or kernel execution.

device_id.description: CUDA device index. Supports multi-GPU workloads.

src_address.description: Source memory address of the transfer. For Unified Memory events this is the UM page address. Matched against CpuEvent.pinned_address for HARD correlation.

dst_address.description: Destination memory address of the transfer. 0 for kernel and page fault events.

transfer_size_bytes.description: Size of the memory transfer in bytes. Secondary correlation key.

um_page_faults.description: Number of Unified Memory page fault groups for PAGE_FAULT events. 0 for TRANSFER and KERNEL events.

kernel_name.description: CUDA kernel name for KERNEL events; transfer direction label for TRANSFER events. Empty for PAGE_FAULT.

kernel_duration_ns.description: Kernel or transfer execution duration in nanoseconds (CUPTI end - start). 0 for page fault events.

stream_id.description: CUDA stream ID. Distinguishes concurrent kernels and transfers across streams.

device_mem_used_mb.description: GPU device memory in use (MB) at flush time, from cuMemGetInfo. 0 if unavailable.

um_bytes_htod.description: Unified Memory bytes migrated host-to-device for this event. Non-zero only for UM TRANSFER events with HOST_TO_DEVICE kind.

um_bytes_dtoh.description: Unified Memory bytes migrated device-to-host for this event. Non-zero only for UM TRANSFER events with DEVICE_TO_HOST kind.

grid.description: CUDA kernel grid dimensions. {x:0, y:0, z:0} for non-kernel events.

block.description: CUDA kernel block (thread block) dimensions. {x:0, y:0, z:0} for non-kernel events.

registers_per_thread.description: Number of registers used per thread by this kernel. 0 for non-kernel events.

shared_mem_bytes.description: Total shared memory per block (static + dynamic) in bytes. 0 for non-kernel events.

correlation_id.description: CUPTI correlation ID linking this activity record to its originating CUDA API call. 0 if unavailable.

CorrelatedEvent.description: Output of the correlator. Links a CpuEvent to a GpuEvent with a confidence score.

cpu_event_id.description: References BaseEvent.event_id of the matched CpuEvent.

gpu_event_id.description: References BaseEvent.event_id of the matched GpuEvent.

confidence.description: HARD = address match confirmed. WEAK = timestamp + size match only.

match_reason.description: Which correlation keys produced this match.

latency_ns.description: Time between the CpuEvent and GpuEvent timestamps in nanoseconds. Represents CPU-to-GPU transfer latency.

ProfilingSession.description: Top-level container for a complete profiling run. This is the JSON file the web UI loads.

session_id.description: Unique identifier for this profiling session, e.g. a UUID.

schema_version.description: Schema version string, e.g. '0.1'. Allows the UI to handle future schema changes.

start_time_ns.description: Session start wall-clock time in nanoseconds.

end_time_ns.description: Session end wall-clock time in nanoseconds.

cpu_events.description: All CPU memory events captured during the session.

gpu_events.description: All GPU memory events captured during the session.

correlated_events.description: All matched CPU-GPU event pairs produced by the correlator.
